channel.title: Gpu-Computing on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Gpu-Computing on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted Distributed Training: Horovod vs DeepSpeed vs PyTorch FSDP Guide 2026
item.description: When a single GPU can no longer keep up with model training workloads, distributed training becomes essential. Whether you’re running large-scale recommendation systems, computer vision pipelines, or natural language processing models on a self-hosted GPU cluster, choosing the right distributed training framework determines your hardware utilization, training speed, and operational complexity.

item.title: Self-Hosted GPU Management in Kubernetes: NVIDIA GPU Operator vs Container Toolkit vs Volcano Guide 2026
item.description: Running GPU workloads on Kubernetes requires more than just installing GPU drivers. You need device discovery, driver installation, container runtime integration, resource scheduling, and optionally Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) partitioning. The tooling ecosystem has evolved from manual driver installation on every node to fully automated operators that manage the entire GPU lifecycle.

item.title: Self-Hosted Hyperparameter Optimization: Optuna vs Ray Tune vs Hyperopt Guide 2026
item.description: Finding the right hyperparameters for a model can make the difference between mediocre and state-of-the-art performance. Manual tuning is slow and error-prone. Automated hyperparameter optimization (HPO) frameworks systematically search the parameter space, finding better configurations with fewer training runs.
